# tsParticles - v3.0.0

tsParticles - v3.0.0
====================

[![banner](https://particles.js.org/images/banner3.png)](https://particles.js.org/)

tsParticles - TypeScript Particles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:tsparticles---typescript-particles)

=====================================================================================================================

**A lightweight TypeScript library for creating particles. Dependency free (\*), browser ready and compatible with React.js, Vue.js (2.x and 3.x), Angular, Svelte, jQuery, Preact, Inferno, Riot.js, Solid.js, and Web Components**

Do you want to use it on your website?[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:do-you-want-to-use-it-on-your-website)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_Documentation and Development references [here](https://particles.js.org/docs/)
 📖_

**This library is available on two of the most popular CDNs and it's easy and ready to use, if you were using particles.js it's even easier**.

You'll find the instructions [below](https://github.com/matteobruni/tsparticles/blob/main/README.md#library-installation)
, with all the links you need, and _don't be scared by **TypeScript**, it's just the source language_.

**The output files are just JavaScript**. 🤩

CDNs and `npm` have all the sources you need in **Javascript**, a bundle browser ready (tsparticles.engine.min.js), and all files splitted for `import` syntax.

**If you are interested** there are some _simple instructions_ just [below](https://github.com/matteobruni/tsparticles/blob/main/README.md#library-installation)
 to guide you to migrate from the old particles.js library.

**_Library installation_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:library-installation)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### **_Hosting / CDN_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:hosting--cdn)

**_Please use these hosts or your own to load tsParticles on your projects_**

#### jsDelivr[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:jsdelivr)

### **_npm_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:npm)

_tsParticles Confetti_

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/tsparticles-confetti?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-confetti)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/tsparticles-confetti?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-confetti)

_tsParticles Engine_

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/tsparticles-engine?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-engine)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/tsparticles-engine?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-engine)

_tsParticles Fireworks_

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/tsparticles-fireworks?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-fireworks)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/tsparticles-fireworks?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-fireworks)

_tsParticles Slim_

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/tsparticles-slim?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-slim)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/tsparticles-slim?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-slim)

_tsParticles_

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/tsparticles?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/tsparticles?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles)

    npm install tsparticles-engine
    

### **_yarn_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:yarn)

    yarn add tsparticles-engine
    

### **_pnpm_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:pnpm)

    pnpm install tsparticles-engine
    

#### Import and require[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:import-and-require)

    const tsParticles = require("tsparticles-engine");// orimport { tsParticles } from "tsparticles-engine";
    

The imported `tsParticles` is the same instance you have when including the script in the page using the `<script>` tag.

* * *

### **_Usage_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:usage)

Load tsParticles and configure the particles:

[![tsParticles demo](https://media.giphy.com/media/ftHwBpp3b0qNyCXRuu/giphy.gif)](https://particles.js.org/)
 [![tsParticles Confetti demo](https://media.giphy.com/media/ftHwBpp3b0qNyCXRuu/giphy.gif)](https://confetti.js.org/)

**index.html**

    <div id="tsparticles"></div><script src="tsparticles.engine.min.js"></script>
    

**app.js**

    /* tsParticles.load(@params); */tsParticles.load({    id: "tsparticles",    options: {        /* options here */    },    // url: "http://foo.bar/particles.js // this can be used as an alternative to options property});// after initialization this can be used./* tsParticles.setOnClickHandler(@callback); *//* this will be fired from all particles loaded */tsParticles.setOnClickHandler((event, particles) => {    /* custom on click handler */});// now you can control the animations too, it's possible to pause and resume the animations// these methods don't change the config so you're safe with all your configurations// domItem(0) returns the first tsParticles instance loaded in the domconst particles = tsParticles.domItem(0);// play will start the animations, if the move is not enabled it won't enable it, it just updates the frameparticles.play();// pause will stop the animationsparticles.pause();

### Elementor[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:elementor)

Actually, an official tsParticles plugin isn't existing, but I have a collaboration with the `Premium Addons for Elementor` plugin collection.

![Premium Addons for Elementor](https://particles.js.org/images/premium-addons-wordpress-plugin.png)

Premium Addons for Elementor is one of the most common plugins for Elementor that offers more than 55 highly customizable Elementor Widgets and Section Add-ons. tsParticles is exclusively included in Premium Particles Section Add-on for Elementor Page Builder. [Check It Now](https://premiumaddons.com/particles-section-addon-for-elementor-page-builder/)
.  
Use Premium Addons for Elementor Page Builder and get the chance to include tsParticles in your next WordPress website without the need to write a single line of code. [See a Live Example](https://premiumaddons.com/particles-section-addon-for-elementor-page-builder/)
.

* * *

Presets[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:presets)

---------------------------------------------------------------

There are some presets ready to be used in this repository, and they also have a bundle file that contains everything needed to run.

### Big Circles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:big-circles)

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/tsparticles-preset-big-circles/badge)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/tsparticles-preset-big-circles)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/tsparticles-preset-big-circles.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-preset-big-circles)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/tsparticles-preset-big-circles)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-preset-big-circles)

This preset loads big colored circles moving upwards on a white background.

[![demo](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/raw/main/presets/bigCircles/images/sample.png)](https://particles.js.org/samples/presets/bigCircles)

You can find the instructions [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/tree/main/presets/bigCircles#readme)

Templates and Resources[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:templates-and-resources)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can find some tsParticles related templates [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/templates)
. The templates are created for _Vanilla Javascript_, _ReactJS_, _VueJS_, _Angular_, _SvelteJS_, and other frameworks.

The templates will vary, new ones can be created or older ones updated with the latest features or changed to a better style. Check them out once in a while.

If you created some good design with _tsParticles_ feel free to submit a pull request with your cool template, you'll be credited as the template author!

[https://github.com/tsparticles/templates](https://github.com/tsparticles/templates)

**_Options_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:options)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

You can find all options available [here](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html)
 📖

Want to see it in action and try it?[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:want-to-see-it-in-action-and-try-it)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I've created a tsParticles collection on [CodePen](https://codepen.io/collection/DPOage)
 😮 or you can check out my [profile](https://codepen.io/matteobruni)

Otherwise, there's the demo page link below.

[![tsParticles demo](https://media.giphy.com/media/fsVN1ZHksgBIXNIbr1/giphy.gif)](https://particles.js.org/samples/)

Want to see even more demos? Clone the repository on your computer and follow these instructions

    $ pnpm i$ pnpm run build$ cd demo/vanilla$ pnpm start
    

**Boom! 💥** [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000/)
 and you can check out other demos.

_If you are brave enough_ you can switch to the `dev` branch for trying the features under development.

* * *

Migrating from Particles.js[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:migrating-from-particlesjs)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**tsParticles** has a package that makes this library 100% compatible with the _particles.js_ configuration.

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/tsparticles-particles.js/badge?style=rounded)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/tsparticles-particles.js)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/tsparticles-particles.js.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-particles.js)
 [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/dm/tsparticles-particles.js)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tsparticles-particles.js)

Seriously, you just need to change the script from particles.js to the bundled compatibility package, et-voilà, **you're ready** 🧙!

You can read more **[here](https://dev.to/matteobruni/migrating-from-particles-js-to-tsparticles-2a6m)
**

Want to know 5 reasons to do the switch? [Read here](https://dev.to/matteobruni/5-reasons-to-use-tsparticles-and-not-particles-js-1gbe)

_Below you can find all the information you need to install tsParticles and its new syntax._

* * *

Plugins/Customizations[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:pluginscustomizations)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

tsParticles now supports some customizations 🥳.

**You can create your own plugins**

_Read more [here](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/Core_Interfaces_IPlugin.html)
..._

* * *

Dependency Graphs[](https://particles.js.org/docs/index.html#md:dependency-graphs)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    flowchart LR
    
    subgraph b [Bundles]
    bp[Particles.js compatibility bundle] --> bs[tsParticles Slim]
    bs --> bf[tsParticles]
    end
    
    e[tsParticles Engine] --> b
    
    iea & iebo & iebu & iec & ieg & iepa & iepu & ierem & ierep & ies --> bs
    ipa & ipc & ipl --> bs
    mb & mp --> bs
    ple4 --> bs
    sc & si & sl & sp & ssq & sst & st --> bs
    ua & uc & ul & uop & uou & usi & ust --> bs
    
    iet --> bf
    pla & plem --> bf
    ur & uti & utw & uw --> bf
    
    subgraph i [Interactions]
    
    subgraph ie [Externals]
    iea[Attract]
    iebo[Bounce]
    iebu[Bubble]
    iec[Connect]
    ieg[Grab]
    iepa[Pause]
    iepu[Push]
    ierem[Remove]
    ierep[Repulse]
    ies[Slow]
    iet[Trail]
    end
    
    il[Light]
    
    subgraph ip [Particles]
    ipa[Attract]
    ipc[Collisions]
    ipl[Links]
    ipr[Repulse]
    end
    
    end
    
    i --> ie
    i --> ip
    
    e --> i
    
    subgraph m [Movers]
    mb[Base]
    mp[Parallax]
    end
    
    e --> m
    
    subgraph pa [Paths]
    pac[Curves]
    pape[Perlin Noise]
    papo[Polygon]
    pas[Simplex Noise]
    end
    
    e --> pa
    
    subgraph pl [Plugins]
    pla[Absorbers]
    plem[Emitters]
    plh[HSV Color]
    pli[Infection]
    plm[Motion]
    plp[Polygon Mask]
    
    subgraph plea [Easings]
    pleb[Back]
    pleci[Circ]
    plecu[Cubic]
    plee[Expo]
    ple4[Quad]
    ple5[Quint]
    ples[Sine]
    end
    
    end
    
    pl --> plea
    
    e --> pl
    
    subgraph s [Shapes]
    sb[Bubble]
    sc[Circle]
    sh[Heart]
    si[Image]
    sl[Line]
    sm[Multiline Text]
    sp[Polygon]
    sr[Rounded Rectangle]
    ssp[Spiral]
    ssq[Square]
    sst[Star]
    st[Text]
    end
    
    e --> s
    
    subgraph u [Updaters]
    ua[Angle]
    uc[Color]
    ud[Destroy]
    ug[Gradient]
    ul[Life]
    uop[Opacity]
    uor[Orbit]
    uou[Out Modes]
    ur[Roll]
    usi[Size]
    ust[Stroke Color]
    uti[Tilt]
    utw[Twinkle]
    uw[Wobble]
    end
    
    e --> u

# IOptions | tsParticles - v3.0.0

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)
    
*   [tsParticles Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html)
    
*   [Options/Interfaces/IOptions](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.html)
    
*   [IOptions](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html)
    

Interface IOptions
==================

The Options interface, defines all the options that can be used by `tsParticles` \[\[include:Options.md\]\]

interface IOptions {  
    [autoPlay](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#autoPlay)
: boolean;  
    [background](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#background)
: [IBackground](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html)
;  
    [backgroundMask](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#backgroundMask)
: [IBackgroundMask](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html)
;  
    [clear](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#clear)
: boolean;  
    [delay](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#delay)
: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)
;  
    [detectRetina](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#detectRetina)
: boolean;  
    [duration](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#duration)
: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)
;  
    [fpsLimit](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)
: number;  
    [fullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fullScreen)
: boolean | [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<[IFullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html)
\>;  
    [interactivity](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#interactivity)
: [IInteractivity](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Interactivity_IInteractivity.IInteractivity.html)
;  
    [key](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#key)
?: string;  
    [manualParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#manualParticles)
: [IManualParticle](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IManualParticle.IManualParticle.html)
\[\];  
    [name](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#name)
?: string;  
    [particles](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#particles)
: [IParticlesOptions](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_IParticlesOptions.IParticlesOptions.html)
;  
    [pauseOnBlur](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#pauseOnBlur)
: boolean;  
    [pauseOnOutsideViewport](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#pauseOnOutsideViewport)
: boolean;  
    [preset](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#preset)
?: [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
<string\>;  
    [responsive](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#responsive)
: [IResponsive](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IResponsive.IResponsive.html)
\[\];  
    [smooth](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#smooth)
: boolean;  
    [style](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#style)
: [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<CSSStyleDeclaration\>;  
    [themes](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#themes)
: [ITheme](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_ITheme.ITheme.html)
\[\];  
    [zLayers](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#zLayers)
: number;  
    \[name: string\]: unknown;  
}

*   \[name: string\]: unknown
    
    More custom options for external plugins or customizations

Properties
----------

### autoPlay[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#autoPlay)

autoPlay: boolean

Sets if the animations should start automatically or manually

### background[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#background)

background: [IBackground](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html)

Background options, these background options will be used to the canvas element, they are all CSS properties

### backgroundMask[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#backgroundMask)

backgroundMask: [IBackgroundMask](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html)

Background Mask options, what's behind the canvas will become hidden and particles will uncover it

### clear[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#clear)

clear: boolean

Clears the canvas on every frame if enabled

### delay[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#delay)

delay: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)

The initial delay before starting the animation

### detectRetina[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#detectRetina)

detectRetina: boolean

Enables the retina detection, if disabled the ratio used by canvas will be always 1 and not the device setting.

### duration[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#duration)

duration: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)

The Particles effect duration in seconds, then the container will be destroyed

### fpsLimit[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)

fpsLimit: number

The FPS (Frame Per Second) limit applied to all particles animations.

### fullScreen[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fullScreen)

fullScreen: boolean | [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<[IFullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html)
\>

Sets the animated background mode for particles canvas bringing it to the back

### interactivity[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#interactivity)

interactivity: [IInteractivity](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Interactivity_IInteractivity.IInteractivity.html)

The particles interaction options

### `Optional`key[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#key)

key?: string

The key used to store the options in the Engine.configs collection

### manualParticles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#manualParticles)

manualParticles: [IManualParticle](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IManualParticle.IManualParticle.html)
\[\]

Particles inserted at load time with a specific position

### `Optional`name[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#name)

name?: string

The name of the config, if it needs to be shown to the user, used also if key is missing

### particles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#particles)

particles: [IParticlesOptions](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_IParticlesOptions.IParticlesOptions.html)

The particles options

### pauseOnBlur[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#pauseOnBlur)

pauseOnBlur: boolean

Enables or disabled the animation on window blur

### pauseOnOutsideViewport[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#pauseOnOutsideViewport)

pauseOnOutsideViewport: boolean

Enable or disabled the animation if the element is outside the viewport

### `Optional`preset[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#preset)

preset?: [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
<string\>

This property will be used to add specified presets to the options

### responsive[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#responsive)

responsive: [IResponsive](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IResponsive.IResponsive.html)
\[\]

This sets custom options based on canvas size

### smooth[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#smooth)

smooth: boolean

Enables a smooth effect, by default it's disabled When enabled the animation will speed up or slow down depending on fps The [IOptions.fpsLimit](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)
 field will be used as a reference for the animation speed Some examples: - with a [IOptions.fpsLimit](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)
 of 60 the animation will be twice faster on 120 fps devices - with a [IOptions.fpsLimit](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)
 of 120 the animation will be twice slower on 60 fps devices The animation will be always smooth, but the behavior could be affected by the user screen refresh rate It's recommended to keep this disabled, be careful.

### style[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#style)

style: [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<CSSStyleDeclaration\>

### themes[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#themes)

themes: [ITheme](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_ITheme.ITheme.html)
\[\]

User-defined themes that can be retrieved by the particles [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)

### zLayers[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#zLayers)

zLayers: number

The maximum layers used in the z-axis

# IFullScreen | tsParticles - v3.0.0

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)
    
*   [tsParticles Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html)
    
*   [Options/Interfaces/FullScreen/IFullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.html)
    
*   [IFullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html)
    

Interface IFullScreen
=====================

The options to set the particles in the background using CSS `fixed` position The [IFullScreen.zIndex](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html#zIndex)
 property sets the background CSS `z-index` property \[\[include:Options/FullScreen.md\]\]

interface IFullScreen {  
    [enable](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html#enable)
: boolean;  
    [zIndex](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html#zIndex)
: number;  
}

#### Implemented by

*   [FullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html)
    

##### Index

### Properties

[enable](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html#enable)
 [zIndex](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html#zIndex)

Properties
----------

### enable[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html#enable)

enable: boolean

This property sets the canvas to a full window size acting like a background, the most common configuration. The default value is `false` since many already have different configurations.

This is really helpful since there's no need to write CSS code to have a full size tsParticles instance.

### zIndex[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html#zIndex)

zIndex: number

This is the CSS `z-index` property set to the canvas.

If the `z-index` is less than `0` the mouse interactions works only with the `interactivity.detectsOn` set to `window`.

*   [Background](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html)
    

##### Index

### Properties

[color](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#color)
 [image](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#image)
 [opacity](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#opacity)
 [position](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#position)
 [repeat](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#repeat)
 [size](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#size)

Properties
----------

### color[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#color)

color: string | [IRangeColor](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IRangeColor.html)

The `color` property can be set to a HEX string or to a [color object](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionsColor.IOptionsColor.html)
, that is the same as the one used in `particles.color` options.

This color is set to canvas style `background-color` property, if this property is not set the background will be transparent.

### image[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#image)

image: string

The `image` property sets the canvas style `background-image` property.

This property doesn't have a default value, anyway if you need a background image you need to specify the same CSS syntax with the `url()` function.

### opacity[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#opacity)

opacity: number

The `opacity` property sets the `color` property opacity, so you can set a semi-transparent background.

This value is by default to `1` and it accepts any value between `0` and `1` included.

### position[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#position)

position: string

The `position` property sets the canvas style `background-position` property.

This [link](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/background-position)
 can be useful to set the right value to this property.

### repeat[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#repeat)

repeat: string

The `repeat` property sets the canvas style `background-repeat` property.

This [link](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/background-repeat)
 can be useful to set the right value to this property.

### size[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html#size)

size: string

The `size` property sets the canvas style `background-size` property.

This [link](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/background-size)
 can be useful to set the right value to this property.

### Settings

# IBackgroundMask | tsParticles - v3.0.0

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)
    
*   [tsParticles Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html)
    
*   [Options/Interfaces/BackgroundMask/IBackgroundMask](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.html)
    
*   [IBackgroundMask](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html)
    

Interface IBackgroundMask
=========================

The options to apply a base color to canvas to cover what's behind The particles will unveil what is covered by the canvas \[\[include:Options/BackgroundMask.md\]\]

interface IBackgroundMask {  
    [composite](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#composite)
: GlobalCompositeOperation;  
    [cover](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#cover)
: string | [IColor](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IColor.html)
 | [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<[IBackgroundMaskCover](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMaskCover.IBackgroundMaskCover.html)
\>;  
    [enable](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#enable)
: boolean;  
}

#### Implemented by

*   [BackgroundMask](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_BackgroundMask_BackgroundMask.BackgroundMask.html)
    

##### Index

### Properties

[composite](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#composite)
 [cover](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#cover)
 [enable](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#enable)

Properties
----------

### composite[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#composite)

composite: GlobalCompositeOperation

This property is used to choose the composition mode for the background mask effect.

The default value is `destination-out`, which unveils the background below using drawn elements, any other valid value can be found [here](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CanvasRenderingContext2D/globalCompositeOperation)

### cover[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#cover)

cover: string | [IColor](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IColor.html)
 | [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<[IBackgroundMaskCover](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMaskCover.IBackgroundMaskCover.html)
\>

The `cover` property can be set to a HEX string or to a [color object](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IColor.html)
, that is the same as the one used in `particles.color` options.

The `cover` can also be a [cover object](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMaskCover.IBackgroundMaskCover.html)
 like the one described below.

### enable[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_BackgroundMask_IBackgroundMask.IBackgroundMask.html#enable)

enable: boolean

This property set the background mask mode, this mode enables the `composite` option to all elements drawn.

### detectsOn[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Interactivity_IInteractivity.IInteractivity.html#detectsOn)

detectsOn:  
    | "window"  
    | "canvas"  
    | "parent"  
    | [InteractivityDetect](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_InteractivityDetect.InteractivityDetect.html)

Where the mouse events will be detected If set to `canvas` only the particles canvas will be targeted If set to `parent` only the particles canvas parent will be targeted If set to `window` every area will be the target

### events[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Interactivity_IInteractivity.IInteractivity.html#events)

events: [IEvents](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Interactivity_Events_IEvents.IEvents.html)

Interaction events options, this configures which events are enabled and which modes should be used

### modes[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Interactivity_IInteractivity.IInteractivity.html#modes)

modes: [IModes](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Interactivity_Modes_IModes.IModes.html)

Interaction modes options, this configures every mode behavior

Properties
----------

### autoPlay[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#autoPlay)

autoPlay: boolean

Sets if the animations should start automatically or manually

### `Readonly`background[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#background)

background: [Background](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html)

Background options, these background options will be used to the canvas element, they are all CSS properties

### `Readonly`backgroundMask[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#backgroundMask)

backgroundMask: [BackgroundMask](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_BackgroundMask_BackgroundMask.BackgroundMask.html)

Background Mask options, what's behind the canvas will become hidden and particles will uncover it

### clear[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#clear)

clear: boolean

Clears the canvas on every frame if enabled

### defaultThemes[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#defaultThemes)

defaultThemes: DefaultThemes

### delay[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#delay)

delay: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)

The initial delay before starting the animation

### detectRetina[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#detectRetina)

detectRetina: boolean

Enables the retina detection, if disabled the ratio used by canvas will be always 1 and not the device setting.

### duration[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#duration)

duration: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)

The Particles effect duration in seconds, then the container will be destroyed

### fpsLimit[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#fpsLimit)

fpsLimit: number

The FPS (Frame Per Second) limit applied to all particles animations.

### `Readonly`fullScreen[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#fullScreen)

fullScreen: [FullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html)

Sets the animated background mode for particles canvas bringing it to the back

### `Readonly`interactivity[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#interactivity)

interactivity: [Interactivity](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Interactivity_Interactivity.Interactivity.html)

The particles interaction options

### `Optional`key[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#key)

key?: string

The key used to store the options in the Engine.configs collection

### manualParticles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#manualParticles)

manualParticles: [ManualParticle](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_ManualParticle.ManualParticle.html)
\[\]

Particles inserted at load time with a specific position

### `Optional`name[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#name)

name?: string

The name of the config, if it needs to be shown to the user, used also if key is missing

### `Readonly`particles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#particles)

particles: [ParticlesOptions](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Particles_ParticlesOptions.ParticlesOptions.html)

The particles options

### pauseOnBlur[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#pauseOnBlur)

pauseOnBlur: boolean

Enables or disabled the animation on window blur

### pauseOnOutsideViewport[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#pauseOnOutsideViewport)

pauseOnOutsideViewport: boolean

Enable or disabled the animation if the element is outside the viewport

### `Optional`preset[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#preset)

preset?: [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
<string\>

This property will be used to add specified presets to the options

### responsive[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#responsive)

responsive: [Responsive](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Responsive.Responsive.html)
\[\]

This sets custom options based on canvas size

### smooth[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#smooth)

smooth: boolean

Enables a smooth effect, by default it's disabled When enabled the animation will speed up or slow down depending on fps The [IOptions.fpsLimit](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)
 field will be used as a reference for the animation speed Some examples: - with a [IOptions.fpsLimit](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)
 of 60 the animation will be twice faster on 120 fps devices - with a [IOptions.fpsLimit](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html#fpsLimit)
 of 120 the animation will be twice slower on 60 fps devices The animation will be always smooth, but the behavior could be affected by the user screen refresh rate It's recommended to keep this disabled, be careful.

### style[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#style)

style: [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<CSSStyleDeclaration\>

### `Readonly`themes[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#themes)

themes: [Theme](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Theme_Theme.Theme.html)
\[\]

User-defined themes that can be retrieved by the particles [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)

### zLayers[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Options.Options.html#zLayers)

zLayers: number

The maximum layers used in the z-axis

# Container | tsParticles - v3.0.0

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)

Class Container
===============

The object loaded into an HTML element, it'll contain options loaded and all data to let everything working \[\[include:Container.md\]\]

### constructor[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html#constructor)

*   new Container(engine, id, sourceOptions?): [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)
    [](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html#constructor.new_Container)
    
*   This is the core class, create an instance to have a new working particles manager
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   engine: [Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html)
        
        the engine used by container
        
    *   id: string
        
        the id to identify this instance
        
    *   `Optional`sourceOptions: [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
        <[IOptions](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html)
        \>
        
        the options to load
        
    
    #### Returns [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)

# tsParticles Engine - v3.8.1 | tsParticles - v3.0.0

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)
    
*   [tsParticles Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html)
    

Module tsParticles Engine - v3.8.1
==================================

[![banner](https://particles.js.org/images/banner3.png)](https://particles.js.org/)

tsParticles - TypeScript Particles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:tsparticles---typescript-particles)

==========================================================================================================================================

**A lightweight TypeScript library for creating particles. Dependency free (\*), browser ready and compatible with React.js, Vue.js (2.x and 3.x), Angular, Svelte, jQuery, Preact, Inferno, Riot.js, Solid.js, and Web Components**

Do you want to use it on your website?[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:do-you-want-to-use-it-on-your-website)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_Documentation and Development references [here](https://particles.js.org/docs/)
 📖_

**This library is available on two of the most popular CDNs and it's easy and ready to use, if you were using particles.js it's even easier**.

You'll find the instructions [below](https://github.com/tsparticles/tsparticles/blob/main/README.md#library-installation)
, with all the links you need, and _don't be scared by **TypeScript**, it's just the source language_.

**The output files are just JavaScript**. 🤩

CDNs and `npm` have all the sources you need in **Javascript**, a bundle browser ready (tsparticles.engine.min.js), and all files splitted for `import` syntax.

**If you are interested** there are some _simple instructions_ just [below](https://github.com/tsparticles/tsparticles/blob/main/README.md#library-installation)
 to guide you to migrate from the old particles.js library.

**_Library installation_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:library-installation)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### **_yarn_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:yarn)

    yarn add @tsparticles/engine
    

### **_pnpm_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:pnpm)

    pnpm install @tsparticles/engine
    

#### Import and require[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:import-and-require)

    const tsParticles = require("@tsparticles/engine");// orimport { tsParticles } from "@tsparticles/engine";
    

The imported `tsParticles` is the same instance you have when including the script in the page using the `<script>` tag.

* * *

### **_Usage_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:usage)

Load tsParticles and configure the particles:

[![tsParticles demo](https://media.giphy.com/media/ftHwBpp3b0qNyCXRuu/giphy.gif)](https://particles.js.org/)
 [![tsParticles Confetti demo](https://media.giphy.com/media/ftHwBpp3b0qNyCXRuu/giphy.gif)](https://confetti.js.org/)

**index.html**

    <div id="tsparticles"></div><script src="tsparticles.engine.min.js"></script>
    

**app.js**

    // @path-json can be an object or an array, the first will be loaded directly, and the object from the array will be randomly selected/* tsParticles.load(@params); */tsParticles  .load({    id: "tsparticles",    url: "presets/default.json",  })  .then((container) => {    console.log("callback - tsparticles config loaded");  })  .catch((error) => {    console.error(error);  });//ortsParticles.load({  id: "tsparticles",  options: {    /* options here */  },});//ortsParticles.load({  id: "tsparticles",  options: [    {      /* options here */    },    {      /* other options here */    },  ],});//random objecttsParticles.load({  id: "tsparticles",  options: [    {      /* options here */    },    {      /* other options here */    },  ],  index: 1,}); //the second one// Important! If the index is not in range 0...<array.length, the index will be ignored.// after initialization this can be used./* tsParticles.setOnClickHandler(@callback); *//* this will be fired from all particles loaded */tsParticles.setOnClickHandler((event, particles) => {  /* custom on click handler */});// now you can control the animations too, it's possible to pause and resume the animations// these methods don't change the config so you're safe with all your configurations// domItem(0) returns the first tsParticles instance loaded in the domconst particles = tsParticles.domItem(0);// play will start the animations, if the move is not enabled it won't enable it, it just updates the frameparticles.play();// pause will stop the animationsparticles.pause();
    

* * *

### Elementor[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:elementor)

Actually, an official tsParticles plugin isn't existing, but I have a collaboration with the `Premium Addons for Elementor` plugin collection.

![Premium Addons for Elementor](https://particles.js.org/images/premium-addons-wordpress-plugin.png)

Premium Addons for Elementor is one of the most common plugins for Elementor that offers more than 55 highly customizable Elementor Widgets and Section Add-ons. tsParticles is exclusively included in Premium Particles Section Add-on for Elementor Page Builder. [Check It Now](https://premiumaddons.com/particles-section-addon-for-elementor-page-builder/)
.  
Use Premium Addons for Elementor Page Builder and get the chance to include tsParticles in your next WordPress website without the need to write a single line of code. [See a Live Example](https://premiumaddons.com/particles-section-addon-for-elementor-page-builder/)
.

* * *

Presets[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:presets)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are some presets ready to be used in [this repository](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets)
, and they also have a bundle file that contains everything needed to run.

### Big Circles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:big-circles)

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-big-circles/badge)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-big-circles)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/@tsparticles/preset-big-circles.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-big-circles)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/@tsparticles/preset-big-circles)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-big-circles)

This preset loads big colored circles moving upwards on a white background.

[![demo](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/raw/main/presets/bigCircles/images/sample.png)](https://particles.js.org/samples/presets/bigCircles)

You can find the instructions [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/tree/main/presets/bigCircles#readme)

### Bubbles[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:bubbles)

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-bubbles/badge)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/tsparticles)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/@tsparticles/preset-bubbles.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-bubbles)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/@tsparticles/preset-bubbles)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-bubbles)

This preset loads colored bubbles coming from the bottom of the screen on a white background.

[![demo](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/raw/main/presets/bubbles/images/sample.png)](https://particles.js.org/samples/presets/bubbles)

You can find the instructions [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/tree/main/presets/bubbles#readme)

### Confetti[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:confetti)

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-confetti/badge)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-confetti)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/@tsparticles/preset-confetti.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-confetti)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/@tsparticles/preset-confetti)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-confetti)

This preset loads white and red confetti launched from the screen center on a transparent background.

[![demo](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/raw/main/presets/confetti/images/sample.png)](https://particles.js.org/samples/presets/confetti)

You can find the instructions [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/tree/main/presets/confetti#readme)

### Fire[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:fire)

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-fire/badge)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-fire)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/@tsparticles/preset-fire.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-fire)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/@tsparticles/preset-fire)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-fire)

This preset loads a faded red to a black background with particles colored like fire and ash sparks.

[![demo](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/raw/main/presets/fire/images/sample.png)](https://particles.js.org/samples/presets/fire)

You can find the instructions [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/tree/main/presets/fire#readme)

### Firefly[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:firefly)

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-firefly/badge)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/@tsparticles/preset-firefly)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/@tsparticles/preset-firefly.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-firefly)
 [![npmjs](https://img.shields.io/npm/dt/@tsparticles/preset-firefly)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/preset-firefly)

This preset loads a mouse trail made with small fading particles like little fireflies.

[![demo](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/raw/main/presets/firefly/images/sample.png)](https://particles.js.org/samples/presets/firefly)

You can find the instructions [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/presets/tree/main/presets/firefly#readme)

Templates and Resources[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:templates-and-resources)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can find some tsParticles related templates [here](https://github.com/tsparticles/templates)
. The templates are created for _Vanilla Javascript_, _ReactJS_, _VueJS_, _Angular_, _SvelteJS_, and other frameworks.

The templates will vary, new ones can be created or older ones updated with the latest features or changed to a better style. Check them out once in a while.

If you created some good design with _tsParticles_ feel free to submit a pull request with your cool template, you'll be credited as the template author!

[https://github.com/tsparticles/templates](https://github.com/tsparticles/templates)

* * *

**_Options_**[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:options)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can find all options available [here](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html)
 📖

Want to see it in action and try it?[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:want-to-see-it-in-action-and-try-it)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I've created a tsParticles collection on [CodePen](https://codepen.io/collection/DPOage)
 😮 or you can check out my [profile](https://codepen.io/matteobruni)

Otherwise, there's the demo page link below.

[![tsParticles demo](https://media.giphy.com/media/fsVN1ZHksgBIXNIbr1/giphy.gif)](https://particles.js.org/samples/)

Want to see even more demos? Clone the repository on your computer and follow these instructions

    $ pnpm i$ pnpm run build$ cd demo/vanilla$ pnpm start
    

**Boom! 💥** [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000/)
 and you can check out other demos.

_If you are brave enough_ you can switch to the `dev` branch for trying the features under development.

* * *

Migrating from Particles.js[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:migrating-from-particlesjs)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**tsParticles** has a package that makes this library 100% compatible with the _particles.js_ configuration.

[![jsDelivr](https://data.jsdelivr.com/v1/package/npm/@tsparticles/particles.js/badge?style=rounded)](https://www.jsdelivr.com/package/npm/@tsparticles/particles.js)
 [![npmjs](https://badge.fury.io/js/@tsparticles/particles.js.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/particles.js)
 [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/dm/@tsparticles/particles.js)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tsparticles/particles.js)

Seriously, you just need to change the script from particles.js to the bundled compatibility package, et-voilà, **you're ready** 🧙!

You can read more **[here](https://dev.to/matteobruni/migrating-from-particles-js-to-tsparticles-2a6m)
**

Want to know 5 reasons to do the switch? [Read here](https://dev.to/matteobruni/5-reasons-to-use-tsparticles-and-not-particles-js-1gbe)

_Below you can find all the information you need to install tsParticles and its new syntax._

* * *

Plugins/Customizations[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:pluginscustomizations)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

tsParticles now supports some customizations 🥳.

**You can create your own plugins**

_Read more [here](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/Core_Interfaces_IPlugin.html)
..._

* * *

Dependency Graph[](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html#md:dependency-graph)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    flowchart TD
    
      subgraph core [Core]
        engine[tsParticles Engine]
        perlin-noise[Perlin Noise Lib]
        simplex-noise[Simplex Noise Lib]
        configs[tsParticles Configs]
      end
    
      subgraph bundle-basic [tsParticles Basic]
    
        subgraph basic-movers [Movers]
          move-base[Base]
        end
    
        subgraph basic-shapes [Shapes]
          shape-circle[Circle]
        end
    
        subgraph basic-updates [Updaters]
          updater-color[Color]
          updater-opacity[Opacity]
          updater-out-modes[Out Modes]
          updater-size[Size]
        end
    
      end
    
      engine --> bundle-basic
    
      subgraph bundle-confetti [tsParticles Confetti]
    
        subgraph confetti-plugins [Plugins]
          plugin-emitters
          plugin-motion
        end
    
        subgraph confetti-shapes [Shapes]
          shape-cards
          shape-emoji
          shape-heart
          shape-image
          shape-polygon
          shape-square
          shape-star
        end
    
        subgraph confetti-updaters [Updaters]
          updater-life
          updater-roll
          updater-rotate
          updater-tilt
          updater-wobble
        end
    
      end
    
      bundle-basic --> bundle-confetti
    
      subgraph bundle-slim [tsParticles Slim]
    
        subgraph slim-interactions [Interactions]
    
          subgraph slim-interactions-external [Externals]
            interaction-external-attract[Attract]
            interaction-external-bounce[Bounce]
            interaction-external-bubble[Bubble]
            interaction-external-connect[Connect]
            interaction-external-grab[Grab]
            interaction-external-pause[Pause]
            interaction-external-push[Push]
            interaction-external-remove[Remove]
            interaction-external-repulse[Repulse]
            interaction-external-slow[Slow]
          end
    
          subgraph slim-interactions-particles [Particles]
            interaction-particles-attract[Attract]
            interaction-particles-collisions[Collisions]
            interaction-particles-links[Links]
          end
    
        end
    
        subgraph slim-movers [Movers]
          move-parallax[Parallax]
        end
    
        subgraph slim-plugins [Plugins]
    
          subgraph slim-plugins-easings [Easings]
            plugin-easing-quad[Quad]
          end
    
        end
    
        subgraph slim-shapes [Shapes]
          shape-emoji[Emoji]
          shape-image[Image]
          shape-line[Line]
          shape-polygon[Polygon]
          shape-square[Square]
          shape-star[Star]
        end
    
        subgraph slim-updaters [Updaters]
          updater-life[Life]
          updater-rotate[Rotate]
          updater-stroke-color[Stroke Color]
        end
    
      end
    
      bundle-basic --> bundle-slim
    
      subgraph bundle-fireworks [tsParticles Fireworks]
    
        subgraph fireworks-effects [Effects]
          effect-trail
        end
    
        subgraph fireworks-plugins [Plugins]
          plugin-emitters
    
          subgraph fireworks-plugin-emitters-shapes [Emitters Shapes]
            plugin-emitters-shape-square
          end
    
          plugin-sounds
        end
    
        subgraph fireworks-updaters [Updaters]
          updater-destroy
          updater-life
          updater-rotate
        end
    
      end
    
      bundle-basic --> bundle-fireworks
    
      subgraph bundle-full [tsParticles]
    
        subgraph full-interactions [Interactions]
    
          subgraph full-interactions-external [Externals]
            interaction-external-trail[Trail]
          end
    
        end
    
        subgraph full-plugins [Plugins]
          plugin-absorbers[Absorbers]
          plugin-emitters[Emitters]
    
          subgraph full-plugin-emitters-shapes [Emitters Shapes]
            plugin-emitters-shape-circle[Circle]
            plugin-emitters-shape-square[Square]
          end
    
        end
    
        subgraph full-shapes [Shapes]
          shape-text[Text]
        end
    
        subgraph full-updaters [Updaters]
          updater-destroy[Destroy]
          updater-roll[Roll]
          updater-tilt[Tilt]
          updater-twinkle[Twinkle]
          updater-wobble[Wobble]
        end
    
      end
    
      bundle-slim --> bundle-full
    
      subgraph bundle-all [tsParticles All]
    
        bundle-pjs[tsParticles Particles.js Compatibility]
    
        subgraph all-effects [Effects]
          effect-bubble[Bubble]
          effect-trail[Trail]
        end
    
        subgraph all-interactions [Interactions]
          subgraph all-interactions-external [External]
            interaction-external-particle[Particle]
            interaction-external-pop[Pop]
          end
    
          interaction-light[Light]
    
          subgraph all-interactions-particles [Particles]
            interaction-particles-repulse[Repulse]
          end
        end
    
        subgraph all-paths [Paths]
          path-curl-noise[Curl Noise]
          path-curves[Curves]
          path-perlin-noise[Perlin Noise]
          path-polygon[Polygon]
          path-simplex-noise[Simplex Noise]
          path-svg[SVG]
        end
    
        subgraph all-plugins [Plugins]
          plugin-canvas-mask[Canvas Mask]
    
          subgraph all-plugins-colors [Colors]
            plugin-hsv-color[HSV Color]
            plugin-named-color[Named Color]
            plugin-oklch-color[Oklch Color]
          end
    
          subgraph all-plugins-easings [Easings]
            plugin-easing-back[Back]
            plugin-easing-circ[Circ]
            plugin-easing-cubic[Cubic]
            plugin-easing-expo[Expo]
            plugin-easing-linear[Linear]
            plugin-easing-quart[Quart]
            plugin-easing-quint[Quint]
            plugin-easing-sine[Sine]
          end
    
          subgraph all-plugin-emitters-shapes [Emitters Shapes]
            plugin-emitters-shape-canvas[Canvas]
            plugin-emitters-shape-path[Path]
            plugin-emitters-shape-polygon[Polygon]
          end
    
          subgraph all-plugins-exports [Exports]
            plugin-export-image[Image]
            plugin-export-json[JSON]
            plugin-export-video[Video]
          end
    
          plugin-infection[Infection]
          plugin-motion[Motion]
          plugin-poisson-disc[Poisson Disc]
          plugin-polygon-mask[Polygon Mask]
          plugin-sounds[Sounds]
        end
    
        subgraph all-shapes [Shapes]
          shape-arrow[Arrow]
          shape-cards[Cards]
          shape-cog[Cog]
          shape-heart[Heart]
          shape-path[Path]
          shape-rounded-polygon[Rounded Polygon]
          shape-rounded-rect[Rounded Rect]
          shape-spiral[Spiral]
        end
    
        subgraph all-updaters [Updaters]
          updater-gradient[Gradient]
          updater-orbit[Orbit]
        end
    
        simplex-noise --> path-curl-noise
        perlin-noise --> path-perlin-noise
        simplex-noise --> path-simplex-noise
    
      end
    
      bundle-full --> bundle-all

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)
    
*   [tsParticles Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html)

*   [IThemeDefault](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html)
    

Interface IThemeDefault
=======================

Options to set the default theme

interface IThemeDefault {  
    [auto](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#auto)
: boolean;  
    [mode](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#mode)
:  
        | "any"  
        | "dark"  
        | "light"  
        | [ThemeMode](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_Modes_ThemeMode.ThemeMode.html)
;  
    [value](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#value)
: boolean;  
}

#### Implemented by

*   [ThemeDefault](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Theme_ThemeDefault.ThemeDefault.html)
    

##### Index

### Properties

[auto](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#auto)
 [mode](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#mode)
 [value](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#value)

Properties
----------

### auto[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#auto)

auto: boolean

If set to true, this theme will be switched when the mode matches the user OS theme

### mode[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#mode)

mode:  
    | "any"  
    | "dark"  
    | "light"  
    | [ThemeMode](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_Modes_ThemeMode.ThemeMode.html)

Default theme mode `any`: Sets the default theme for every configuration `dark`: Sets the default theme when the user has dark mode set `light`: Sets the default theme when the user has light mode set If `dark` or `light` themes are set, the `any` default theme will be used only for unset modes

### value[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Theme_IThemeDefault.IThemeDefault.html#value)

value: boolean

Sets or unsets the default flag to this theme and mode

*   External

# FullScreen | tsParticles - v3.0.0

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)
    
*   [tsParticles Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html)
    
*   [Options/Classes/FullScreen/FullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.html)
    
*   [FullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html)
    

Class FullScreen
================

The options to set the particles in the background using CSS `fixed` position The [FullScreen.zIndex](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#zIndex)
 property sets the background CSS `z-index` property \[\[include:Options/FullScreen.md\]\]

#### Implements

*   [IFullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html)
    
*   [IOptionLoader](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html)
    <[IFullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html)
    \>

##### Index

### Constructors

[constructor](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#constructor)

### Properties

[enable](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#enable)
 [zIndex](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#zIndex)

### Methods

[load](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#load)

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#constructor)

*   new FullScreen(): [FullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html)
    [](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#constructor.new_FullScreen)
    
*   #### Returns [FullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html)
    

Properties
----------

### enable[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#enable)

enable: boolean

This property sets the canvas to a full window size acting like a background, the most common configuration. The default value is `false` since many already have different configurations.

This is really helpful since there's no need to write CSS code to have a full size tsParticles instance.

### zIndex[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#zIndex)

zIndex: number

This is the CSS `z-index` property set to the canvas.

If the `z-index` is less than `0` the mouse interactions works only with the `interactivity.detectsOn` set to `window`.

Methods
-------

### load[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#load)

*   load(data?): void[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_FullScreen_FullScreen.FullScreen.html#load.load-1)
    
*   This load method is used to load data in the classes used as generic
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional`data: [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
        <[IFullScreen](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_FullScreen_IFullScreen.IFullScreen.html)
        \>
        
        the input options
        
    
    #### Returns void

*   External

Class Background
================

\[\[include:Options/Background.md\]\]

#### Implements

*   [IBackground](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html)
    
*   [IOptionLoader](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html)
    <[IBackground](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Background_IBackground.IBackground.html)

[constructor](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#constructor)

### Properties

 [image](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#image)

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#constructor)

*   new Background(): [Background](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html)
    [](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#constructor.new_Background)
    
*   #### Returns [Background](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html)
    

Properties
----------

### color[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#color)

color: [OptionsColor](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_OptionsColor.OptionsColor.html)

The `color` property can be set to a HEX string or to a [color object](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionsColor.IOptionsColor.html)
, that is the same as the one used in `particles.color` options.

This color is set to canvas style `background-color` property, if this property is not set the background will be transparent.

### image[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#image)

image: string

The `image` property sets the canvas style `background-image` property.

This property doesn't have a default value, anyway if you need a background image you need to specify the same CSS syntax with the `url()` function.

### opacity[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#opacity)

opacity: number

The `opacity` property sets the `color` property opacity, so you can set a semi-transparent background.

This value is by default to `1` and it accepts any value between `0` and `1` included.

### position[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#position)

position: string

The `position` property sets the canvas style `background-position` property.

This [link](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/background-position)
 can be useful to set the right value to this property.

### repeat[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#repeat)

repeat: string

The `repeat` property sets the canvas style `background-repeat` property.

This [link](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/background-repeat)
 can be useful to set the right value to this property.

### size[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#size)

size: string

The `size` property sets the canvas style `background-size` property.

This [link](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/background-size)
 can be useful to set the right value to this property.

Methods
-------

### load[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#load)

*   load(data?): void[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Background_Background.Background.html#load.load-1)

        \>
        
        the input options

Class Responsive
================

This interface is used by the options classes to have a [IOptionLoader.load](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html#load)
 method to set all needed properties from source

#### Implements

*   [IResponsive](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IResponsive.IResponsive.html)
    
*   [IOptionLoader](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html)
    <[IResponsive](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IResponsive.IResponsive.html)
    \>
