Interface IAnimatableColor
==========================

The animatable color interface, it provides all the necessary properties to create a color animation \[\[include:Options/Particles/Color.md\]\]

    [animation](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IAnimatableColor.IAnimatableColor.html#animation)

 | [IHslAnimation](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IHslAnimation.IHslAnimation.html)
;  
    [value](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IAnimatableColor.IAnimatableColor.html#value)
: [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
<  
        | [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
<string\>  
        | [IRangeValueColor](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IRangeValueColor.html)
  
        | [IRangeRgb](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IRangeRgb.html)

\>;  
}

value: [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
<  
    | [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
<string\>  
    | [IRangeValueColor](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IRangeValueColor.html)

    | [IRangeHsv](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IRangeHsv.html)
\>

Color value, can be a single or an array of [IValueColor](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IValueColor.html)
, [IRgb](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IRgb.html)
, [IHsl](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_Colors.IHsl.html)

Properties
----------

### detectsOn[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Interactivity_Interactivity.Interactivity.html#detectsOn)

detectsOn:  
    | "window"  
    | "canvas"  
    | "parent"  
    | [InteractivityDetect](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_InteractivityDetect.InteractivityDetect.html)

Where the mouse events will be detected If set to `canvas` only the particles canvas will be targeted If set to `parent` only the particles canvas parent will be targeted If set to `window` every area will be the target

### `Readonly`events[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Interactivity_Interactivity.Interactivity.html#events)

events: [Events](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Interactivity_Events_Events.Events.html)

Interaction events options, this configures which events are enabled and which modes should be used

### `Readonly`modes[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Interactivity_Interactivity.Interactivity.html#modes)

modes: [Modes](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Classes_Interactivity_Modes_Modes.Modes.html)

Interaction modes options, this configures every mode behavior

*   [Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html)
    

Class Engine
============

Engine class for creating the singleton on window. It's a singleton class for initializing [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)
 instances, and for Plugins class responsible for every external feature

### addEffect[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addEffect)

*   addEffect(effect, drawer, refresh?): Promise<void\>[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addEffect.addEffect-1)
    
*   addEffect adds effect to tsParticles, it will be available to all future instances created
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   effect: [SingleOrMultiple](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_SingleOrMultiple.SingleOrMultiple.html)
        <string\>
        
        the effect name
        
    *   drawer: [IEffectDrawer](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_IEffectDrawer.IEffectDrawer.html)
        <[Particle](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Particle.Particle.html)
        \>
        
        the effect drawer function or class instance that draws the effect in the canvas
        
    *   refresh: boolean = true
        
        should refresh the dom after adding the effect
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### addEventListener[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addEventListener)

*   addEventListener(type, listener): void[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addEventListener.addEventListener-1)
    
*   Adds a listener to the specified event

    *   type: string
        
        The event to listen to

        The listener of the specified event

### addInteractor[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addInteractor)

*   addInteractor(name, interactorInitializer, refresh?): Promise<void\>[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addInteractor.addInteractor-1)
    
*   Adds an interaction manager to the current collection
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   name: string
        
        the interaction manager name
        
    *   interactorInitializer: InteractorInitializer
        
        the interaction manager initializer
        
    *   refresh: boolean = true
        
        if true the engine will refresh all the containers
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### addMover[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addMover)

*   addMover(name, moverInitializer, refresh?): Promise<void\>[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addMover.addMover-1)

    *   name: string
        
        the mover name
        
    *   moverInitializer: MoverInitializer
        
        the mover initializer
        
    *   refresh: boolean = true
        
        if true the engine will refresh all the containers
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### addParticleUpdater[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addParticleUpdater)

*   addParticleUpdater(name, updaterInitializer, refresh?): Promise<void\>[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addParticleUpdater.addParticleUpdater-1)
    
*   Adds a particle updater to the collection
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   name: string
        
        the particle updater name used as a key
        
    *   updaterInitializer: UpdaterInitializer
        
        the particle updater initializer
        
    *   refresh: boolean = true
        
        if true the engine will refresh all the containers
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### addPathGenerator[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addPathGenerator)

*   addPathGenerator(name, generator, refresh?): Promise<void\>[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addPathGenerator.addPathGenerator-1)
    
*   addPathGenerator adds a named path generator to tsParticles, this can be called by options
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   name: string
        
        the path generator name
        
    *   generator: [IMovePathGenerator](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_IMovePathGenerator.IMovePathGenerator.html)
        
        the path generator object
        
    *   refresh: boolean = true
        
        should refresh the dom after adding the path generator
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### addPlugin[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addPlugin)

*   addPlugin(plugin, refresh?): Promise<void\>[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addPlugin.addPlugin-1)
    
*   addPlugin adds plugin to tsParticles, if an instance needs it, it will be loaded
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   plugin: [IPlugin](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_IPlugin.IPlugin.html)
        
        the plugin implementation of [IPlugin](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_IPlugin.IPlugin.html)
        
    *   refresh: boolean = true
        
        should refresh the dom after adding the plugin
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### addPreset[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addPreset)

*   addPreset(preset, options, override?, refresh?): Promise<void\>[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#addPreset.addPreset-1)
    
*   addPreset adds preset to tsParticles, it will be available to all future instances created
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   preset: string
        
        the preset name
        
    *   options: Readonly<[RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
        <[IOptions](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptions.IOptions.html)
        \>\>
        
        the options to add to the preset
        
    *   override: boolean = false
        
        if true, the preset will override any existing with the same name
        
    *   refresh: boolean = true
        
        should refresh the dom after adding the preset
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>

### domItem[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#domItem)

*   domItem(index): undefined | [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)
    [](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#domItem.domItem-1)
    
*   Retrieves a [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)
     from all the objects loaded
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   index: number
        
        The object index

    The [Container](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Container.Container.html)
     object at specified index, if present or not destroyed, otherwise undefined
    
    #### Deprecated[](https://particles.js.org/docs/classes/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Engine.Engine.html#Deprecated-1)
    
    the domItem() function is deprecated, please use the item function instead

Properties
----------

### angle[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#angle)

angle: number | [IMoveAngle](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMoveAngle.IMoveAngle.html)

The angle value of the particles moving direction, in degrees.

### attract[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#attract)

attract: [IMoveAttract](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMoveAttract.IMoveAttract.html)

The attract options for the particles.

### center[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#center)

center: [ICenterCoordinates](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_ICoordinates.ICenterCoordinates.html)

The center of the particles moving direction. Used when the direction is [MoveDirection.inside](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_Directions_MoveDirection.MoveDirection.html#inside)
 or [MoveDirection.outside](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_Directions_MoveDirection.MoveDirection.html#outside)
, or when [spin](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#spin)
 is enabled.

### decay[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#decay)

decay: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)

The particles movement decay speed.

### direction[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#direction)

direction:  
    | number  
    | "none"  
    | "left"  
    | "top"  
    | "bottom"  
    | "right"  
    | "outside"  
    | "inside"  
    | [MoveDirectionAlt](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_Directions_MoveDirection.MoveDirectionAlt.html)
  
    | [MoveDirection](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_Directions_MoveDirection.MoveDirection.html)
  
    | "bottomLeft"  
    | "bottomRight"  
    | "topLeft"  
    | "topRight"

The particles movement direction, if using a number, it refers to the angle, in degrees.

### distance[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#distance)

distance: number | Partial<[IDistance](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Core_Interfaces_IDistance.IDistance.html)
\>

The particles movement distance.

### drift[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#drift)

drift: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)

The particles movement drift value.

### enable[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#enable)

enable: boolean

The particles movement enable status.

### gravity[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#gravity)

gravity: [IMoveGravity](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMoveGravity.IMoveGravity.html)

The particles movement gravity options.

### outModes[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#outModes)

outModes:  
    | "split"  
    | "none"  
    | "bounce"  
    | "destroy"  
    | "out"  
    | [IOutModes](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IOutModes.IOutModes.html)
  
    | [OutMode](https://particles.js.org/docs/enums/tsParticles_Engine.Enums_Modes_OutMode.OutMode.html)

The particles out modes when leaving canvas bounds.

### path[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#path)

path: [IMovePath](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_Path_IMovePath.IMovePath.html)

The particles path options.

### random[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#random)

random: boolean

Sets if the [speed](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#speed)
 will be randomized or not, maximum value is [speed](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#speed)
.

### size[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#size)

size: boolean

Sets if the particle speed will change based on the particle size, so bigger particles will move faster.

### speed[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#speed)

speed: [RangeValue](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RangeValue.RangeValue.html)

The particles movement speed.

### spin[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#spin)

spin: [ISpin](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_ISpin.ISpin.html)

The particles movement spin options.

### straight[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#straight)

straight: boolean

Sets if the particles direction will be randomized or not, if `true` the value won't be randomized.

### trail[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#trail)

trail: [IMoveTrail](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMoveTrail.IMoveTrail.html)

The particles trail options.

### vibrate[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#vibrate)

vibrate: boolean

Sets if the particles will vibrate or not.

### warp[](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_Particles_Move_IMove.IMove.html#warp)

warp: boolean

Sets if the particle will respawn in the same coordinates on the other side of the canvas, or it will be randomized.

# IOptionLoader | tsParticles - v3.0.0

*   [tsParticles](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules.html)
    
*   [tsParticles Engine](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.html)
    
*   [Options/Interfaces/IOptionLoader](https://particles.js.org/docs/modules/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.html)
    
*   [IOptionLoader](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html)
    

Interface IOptionLoader<T>
==========================

This interface is used by the options classes to have a [IOptionLoader.load](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html#load)
 method to set all needed properties from source

interface IOptionLoader<[T](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html#T)
\> {  
    [load](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html#load.load-1)
(data?: [RecursivePartial](https://particles.js.org/docs/types/tsParticles_Engine.Types_RecursivePartial.RecursivePartial.html)
<[T](https://particles.js.org/docs/interfaces/tsParticles_Engine.Options_Interfaces_IOptionLoader.IOptionLoader.html#T)
\>): void;  
}

#### Type Parameters

*   T
